message:
feat(cli): add server-first dispatch for send, reply, and inbox commands

Restructure the async mail CLI so that send_message, reply_message, and
fetch_inbox first attempt an HTTP tool-call to the running MCP Agent Mail
server before falling back to direct database access. This enables the CLI
to work seamlessly whether the server is running or not:

- send: build JSON payload with to/cc/importance/ack_required/thread_id,
  POST to try_call_server_tool("send_message"), render via emit_output
  on success, otherwise fall through to the existing DB path
- reply: same pattern via "reply_message" server tool, with explicit_to
  forwarding and thread display on success
- inbox: dispatch fetch_inbox with urgent_only/since/limit/include_bodies,
  render table + optional body expansion, fall through on server miss

Server URL and bearer token are resolved from Config::from_env() once at
the top of handle_mail_async, eliminating per-subcommand context
initialization when the server handles the request.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
